title: Misskey
description: Decentralized, ActivityPub-federated microblogging platform with reactions, Drive file storage and a customizable UI.
installation: Misskey is configured PRIMARILY via a YAML file (.config/default.yml), not env vars alone, so before first boot you MUST supply that config (mounted at /misskey/.config) pointing db.host=misskey-postgresql-rw / db.port=5432 / db.db=misskey and redis.host=rfr-misskey-valkey-readwrite / redis.port=6379, and set url to your public https URL. The env vars below mirror those values and cover the parts Misskey reads from the environment; rotate POSTGRES_PASSWORD and the db password in default.yml to match. After the web container is up, run the DB migrations (it runs `pnpm migrate` on start in the official image). COMPANION SERVICES: the single misskey/misskey image runs web + the federation/inbox/deliver/queue workers in one process by default, so no extra worker container is strictly required for small instances; for scale you can run additional misskey/misskey replicas with MK_ONLY_QUEUE/MK_ONLY_SERVER style splits. OPTIONAL: for full-text post search Misskey can use Elasticsearch/OpenSearch or Meilisearch (image getmeili/meilisearch) configured in default.yml under meilisearch/elasticsearch - not provisioned here; and for object storage of Drive uploads it can use any S3-compatible bucket (configured under objectStorage in default.yml) instead of the local volume.
